content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street ■ Suite 620 ■ Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 ■ Fax (916) 322-0886

January 27, 2011


William C. Smith
631 E. Alvin Avenue
Santa Maria, CA 93454

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. W-11-017

Dear Mr. Smith:

     This letter is in response to your request for written advice regarding Government Code
Section 1090 and its application to a potential conflict of interest created by an election to the
Santa Maria Bonita School District Board. Under the provisions of Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D),
copy enclosed, we must decline your request for advice. Government Code Section 1090 is
outside the purview of the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission"), we only
advise on laws pertaining to the Political Reform Act.¹

     If you have any questions about the jurisdiction of the Commission you may wish to
consult our website (www.fppc.ca.gov) or call us at 1-800-ASK-FPPC.

                                             Sincerely,

                                             Scott Hallabrin
                                             General Counsel

                                             By: John W. Wallace
                                                 Assistant General Counsel
                                                 Legal Division
Enclosure


_____________________
     ¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

sections.conclusion: Under Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), the FPPC must decline the request for advice because Government Code Section 1090 is outside the Commission's purview. The FPPC only advises on laws pertaining to the Political Reform Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Government Code Section 1090 creates a conflict of interest for a person elected to the Santa Maria Bonita School District Board.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because Government Code Section 1090 falls outside its jurisdiction; the Commission only advises on matters under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter without formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The document clearly states the reason for declination (lack of jurisdiction over GC § 1090). File number listed as W-11-017, with 'W' potentially indicating withdrawal or written advice request.

QUESTION: Whether Government Code Section 1090 creates a conflict of interest for a person elected to the Santa Maria Bonita School District Board.

CONCLUSION: Under Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), the FPPC must decline the request for advice because Government Code Section 1090 is outside the Commission's purview. The FPPC only advises on laws pertaining to the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide written advice regarding a potential Government Code Section 1090 conflict of interest arising from election to the Santa Maria Bonita School District Board, as this statute is outside the Commission's purview under the Political Reform Act.
